title: Interceptors
description: Per-SUT registry of overlay/popup patterns and their dismissal actions. Consumed by the qtea runtime at Playwright BrowserContext creation to register `page.add_locator_handler()` for each entry, so known overlays (cookie consent, what's-new modals, session-extension prompts) are invisible to every test on every future run. Entries are created via the Step 9 end-of-attempt HITL sweep after a first-encounter overlay blocks a test. The `dismiss.kind` field is a strict whitelist ({click, press_escape}) to close a supply-chain vector: a malicious PR cannot smuggle arbitrary JavaScript via `{"dismiss": {"kind": "evaluate", "script": "..."}}` because the loader rejects any kind outside the whitelist. Location: `<sut>/.qtea/interceptors.json` (shared per-SUT, committed to the repo).

schema_version.description: Schema envelope version. Bump when the entry shape changes incompatibly; loaders reject unknown versions with a warning rather than mis-interpreting.

produced_at.description: When this file was last written. Informational only; the loader does not consult it.

entries.description: One entry per known overlay pattern. Registered order is preserved (first-match on Playwright's side).

overlay.description: How to recognize the overlay when it appears. Uses Playwright's role/name locator vocabulary — never CSS strings (would fail the CLAUDE.md 'no raw DOM / AOM-only' rule) and never XPath.

kind.description: Only role-based overlay matching is supported today. AOM-only per CLAUDE.md.

role.description: The overlay's ARIA role. `dialog`/`alertdialog` cover most modals; `banner`/`region`/`complementary` cover cookie/consent strips; `status`/`alert` cover toast-style interruptions.

name.description: Accessible name to match on. Combined with `name_op` for equals/contains/regex semantics.

name_op.description: How to compare `name`. `equals` is safest (locale-stable). `contains` is useful when the platform pads the name with dynamic tokens. `regex` requires escaping (loader validates the pattern).

frame.description: Where the overlay lives. `top` = main frame only. A URL glob (e.g. `**/onetrust*.com/**`) restricts matching to cross-origin iframes matching that URL — common for OneTrust/TrustArc/Cookiebot widgets. Omit to match in any frame.

dismiss.description: What to do when the overlay appears. `kind` is a strict whitelist — the loader rejects `evaluate` / `fill` / `goto` / `set_localstorage` and any other operation that could smuggle arbitrary code or exfiltrate state. Adding a new kind requires a schema bump and a code change (audited).

kind.description: `click` = click a specific button inside the overlay (safest, most common). `press_escape` = send Escape key to dismiss modals with keyboard support. Deliberately NO `evaluate` (would allow arbitrary JS execution via a PR review that misses one JSON entry). Deliberately NO `fill` or `goto` (out of scope for dismissal; would enable exfiltration or navigation hijack).

target.description: The dismiss control to click. Required when `kind=click`. Same role/name vocabulary as `overlay.*` for consistency. Rejected by the loader when `kind=press_escape`.

role.description: The dismiss control's role. `button` is standard; `link` covers 'skip' or 'not now' text links; `menuitem` covers dismiss controls inside a menu overlay.

name.description: Accessible name of the dismiss control (e.g. 'Reject all', 'Close', 'Not now').

handler_config.description: Playwright `add_locator_handler` configuration. Bounded to prevent runaway registration when a dismiss action reveals another overlay matching the same selector (deadlock scenario).

times.description: Maximum invocations per BrowserContext. On breach, the runtime removes the handler and emits an `overlay_handler_max_reached` event for follow-up HITL. Ceiling of 1000 is a sanity guard; typical value is 100.

no_wait_after.description: Passed to `add_locator_handler(noWaitAfter=...)`. `true` prevents the handler from blocking subsequent actionability checks.

metadata.description: Provenance and audit fields. Not consumed by the runtime — useful for humans reviewing PRs that touch this file.

created_by.description: `hitl` = added via the Step 9 end-of-attempt sweep after operator approved a dismiss action. `manual` = added by a human editing this file directly. No `heuristic` value: heuristic auto-dismisses are logged (audit) but never auto-persisted.

first_seen_test.description: The test id that first triggered this overlay (e.g. `tests/auth/test_login.py::test_login_flow`).

first_seen_url.description: The page URL where this overlay first appeared.

notes.description: Free-text human note (e.g. 'GDPR consent shown on first visit only, gated by NEXT_LOCALE_GDPR cookie').
